channel.title: Read the Room
channel.description: Every Tuesday morning. One topic. Both sides. No bull.

item.title: The Frequency — Issue #8 | Eleven Days.
item.description: New polls show Jared Hudson leading Barry Moore in the Senate runoff. A federal lawsuit challenges Alabama's PSC restructuring. The Science of Reading law took effect June 1. And Valley is building. Five stories on where things stand.

item.title: Signal: What to Know About the Alabama PSC Before June 16
item.description: The Alabama PSC Place 2 runoff on June 16 decides who regulates your power bill. Beeker vs. Zeigler — what you need to know before you vote.

item.title: Read the Room — Issue #9 | Five Words
item.description: The 14th Amendment has said the same thing since 1868. A 2025 executive order says it's been misread for over a century. The Supreme Court is about to tell us which interpretation wins.

item.title: Read the Room — Issue #8 | The Medicaid Argument
item.description: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act restructured Medicaid in ways projected to reduce federal spending by $1 trillion over a decade. The CBO estimates 10.5 million people will lose coverage by 2034.

item.title: Read the Room — Issue #7 | The Warrant Question
Read the Room — Issue #6 | The Map Fight
item.description: Every election has rules. In 2026, both parties are working to write those rules before anyone votes. States are redrawing congressional maps mid-decade at a scale with few modern precedents.

item.title: Read the Room — Issue #5 | The Supreme Court struck down Louisiana's map.
item.description: On April 29, the Supreme Court decided Louisiana v. Callais 6–3. States across the South are already redrawing maps — and analysts say Republicans could gain up to 12 House seats.

item.title: Read the Room — Issue #4 | The courts vs. the White House.
item.description: Hundreds of lawsuits. Dozens of nationwide injunctions. The Trump administration has raced to the Supreme Court's emergency docket at an unprecedented pace — and won roughly 20 of 24 requests.

item.title: Read the Room — Issue #3 | DOGE's cuts are real — but so is the legal resistance.
item.description: DOGE has overseen the departure of an estimated 200,000+ federal workers. Courts have granted relief in roughly 68% of cases where asked. Both sides are digging in.

item.title: Read the Room — Issue #2 | The biggest Medicaid overhaul in decades is underway.
item.description: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act made historic cuts to federal Medicaid financing — projected to reduce federal spending by nearly $1 trillion over ten years. Those cuts are now becoming real.
